action.hypothesis: Round 55 made the optimizer question clear on the strengthened recipe: `gated+adam` with `USE_HADAMARD=1` was not borderline or merely slower, it regressed badly to `0.285949`, so the optimizer branch should be treated as closed and `signum` remains the correct mainline optimizer. The next single question in the prescribed order is therefore loss under the current strengthened anchor: with `ARCHITECTURE=gated`, `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, and `USE_HADAMARD=1` fixed, is the default auxiliary loss still helping, or does removing it improve the full training trajectory now that preprocessing has changed conditioning?
action.summary: Stay on the active `gated` mainline and return to the current signum anchor. Keep `ARCHITECTURE=gated`, `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, and `USE_HADAMARD=1` fixed, and change only the loss-side variable `AUXK_ALPHA` from `0.03125` to `0.0`. Judge this run by the whole curve, not just terminal FVU: if dropping the auxiliary term yields a smoother or lower start/mid/late trajectory than the current `0.142923` anchor, then the strengthened recipe no longer wants the default aux loss; if it is neutral or worse again, restore the default loss and treat the mainline recipe as fully clarified before any further movement.
action.self_review: This stays on the same family, respects the fixed `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` rule, and changes exactly one primary variable: `loss`. It also follows the requested sequence cleanly after the Hadamard-enabled optimizer test failed decisively: there is no justification for an Adam LR callback, so the next informative mainline question is whether the strengthened signum recipe still benefits from the default auxiliary loss.
notes_to_memory: Round 55 strongly rejected `adam` on the Hadamard-enabled `gated@128` anchor: the run finished at `0.285949`, far worse than the `signum` anchor, so the optimizer branch should be treated as closed for the strengthened recipe as well.

Round 56 should isolate the loss question on the current strengthened `gated` anchor by setting `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` at fixed `K=128`, `EF=12`, `signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, and `USE_HADAMARD=1`, evaluating full curve shape rather than endpoint alone.
next_hypotheses: If `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` improves the Hadamard-enabled `gated@128` curve or endpoint, keep loss-side tuning alive with at most one nearby confirmation on the same anchor.
If removing aux is neutral or worse again, restore the default loss and treat the strengthened `gated` mainline recipe as settled before considering any further axis.

result.description: Stay on the active `gated` mainline and return to the current signum anchor. Keep `ARCHITECTURE=gated`, `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, and `USE_HADAMARD=1` fixed, and change only the loss-side variable `AUXK_ALPHA` from `0.03125` to `0.0`. Judge this run by the whole curve, not just terminal FVU: if dropping the auxiliary term yields a smoother or lower start/mid/late trajectory than the current `0.142923` anchor, then the strengthened recipe no longer wants the default aux loss; if it is neutral or worse again, restore the default loss and treat the mainline recipe as fully clarified before any further movement.
result.self_review: This stays on the same family, respects the fixed `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` rule, and changes exactly one primary variable: `loss`. It also follows the requested sequence cleanly after the Hadamard-enabled optimizer test failed decisively: there is no justification for an Adam LR callback, so the next informative mainline question is whether the strengthened signum recipe still benefits from the default auxiliary loss.
